title: Oxymeris sylvestris
intro: Oxymeris sylvestris is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the "wild heliotrope" or "sweet cicely". The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Oxymeris sylvestris include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as bloating and gas.
